Finance AI · P&L
High confidenceOrders + ledgerLast sync: today 06:14
Revenue mix
Organic drives 52% of revenue at the highest margin
Paid channels are declining in efficiency. Dollar for dollar, organic growth is the most profitable path you have right now.
Cost alert
2 recurring costs increased this month
Logistics +15% and a SaaS plan +22%. Together they're $5K/mo — worth a look before they compound into the run rate.
Cash × growth
You can fund ~6 months of scaled acquisition before cash tightens
At a 3.1-month CAC payback and $487K Pure Cash, scaling spend to $46K/mo stays cash-safe for two quarters — the payback returns before the runway runs down.

Revenue → gross margin → CM1 → CM2 → net profit

Cost anomalies — what changed last month
Payroll — overtime+$3,400
Overtime ran higher during the month-end push. Expected $18K, actual $21.4K.
Was this a one-time surge, or is the team structurally understaffed for this volume?
Warehouse — power+$1,600
Cold/secure storage ran longer due to larger inventory held this month.
If that inventory was seasonal, this should normalize next month — worth confirming.
Marketing spend+$5,000
Campaign spend rose across Google (+$3K) and Meta (+$2K) for the seasonal push.
Did the extra spend pay back? Growth Mode below shows whether this scale is profitable.
Growth Mode — can you afford to scale acquisition?
Growth Mode
Finance × Growth · acquisition economics
Room to scaleYour unit economics support spending more on acquisition. CAC pays back in 3.1 months against a 14-month customer lifetime — every $1 of margin can fund growth before it's at risk.
